channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: ☢️🇷🇺🇮🇷 Russia evacuates over 600 employees from Irans Bushehr nuclear plant
item.description: Rosatom chief Likhachev confirms only 20 volunteers remain at Bushehr with 4 in Tehran as Russia completes evacuation of nuclear personnel from Iran amid ongoing conflict. (Source: Anadolu Agency)

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷🇵🇰 Trump says Iran deal to be signed tonight in Islamabad
item.description: President Trump announced an Iran deal would be signed tonight in Islamabad. No Iranian response has been given to Trumps claim yet. (Source: Anadolu Agency)

item.title: 🇳🇴🇪🇺⛽ EU lawmakers urge Norway to share windfall energy profits amid Mideast tensions
item.description: Calls grow for Norway to share increased energy profits as the Iran conflict and fragile ceasefire push prices higher, boosting Norways export earnings. (Source: Anadolu Agency)

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺⚔️ US to delay weapons deliveries to European countries due to Iran war
item.description: Some European nations have been informed that select US weapons deliveries may be delayed as the Iran war continues to draw on American weapons stocks. (Source: Defense News)

item.title: 🛰️🚢📡 Starlink outage hit Navy drone tests exposing Pentagon reliance on SpaceX
item.description: During April 2025 Navy tests involving unmanned boats and drones, Starlink struggled to provide solid network connections, highlighting the Pentagons growing dependence on SpaceX. (Source: Defense News)

item.title: 🇨🇳🇸🇦 China Calls for Hormuz to Stay Open in Xi-Bin Salman Call
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping urged Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman to maintain normal ship passage through the Strait of Hormuz, as Beijing steps up efforts to end the Iran war. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US-Iran Peace Talks Set to Resume as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is expected to travel to Pakistan for the second round of peace negotiations, though Iran has not confirmed its participation as the two-week ceasefire nears its end. (Source: New York Times)

item.title: 🛢️🇺🇸 Trump Invokes Defense Production Act to Expand US Petroleum Output
item.description: President Trump signed a memorandum calling for expanded domestic petroleum production, refining and logistics capacity, citing national defense needs amid the ongoing Iran conflict. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🌊🇦🇪 Gulf States Fear Iran Talks May Cement Tehran Hormuz Control
item.description: Gulf countries worry that US-Iran negotiations might only secure reopening of the Strait of Hormuz while leaving Tehran with enhanced leverage over the critical waterway. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🍎 Apple Names John Ternus as CEO, Tim Cook Becomes Executive Chairman
item.description: Apple appointed insider John Ternus as its next CEO, with Tim Cook transitioning to executive chairman in a major leadership shakeup at the worlds most valuable company. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Sends Mixed Signals on Iran War Path as Ceasefire Nears End
item.description: President Donald Trump gave mixed messages on the path ahead for the U.S. war with Iran, with the ceasefire set to expire Wednesday and prospects for extension described as "highly unlikely." (Source: AP News)

item.title: 🇧🇬 Pro-Russian Ex-Fighter Pilot Rumen Radev Sweeps Bulgaria Election
item.description: Former Bulgarian President Rumen Radev won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections, crushing long-dominant political forces and potentially pushing the EU and NATO member closer to Moscow. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drone Strike on Russian Port of Tuapse Kills One
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse sparked a fire and killed at least one person, Russian officials said, the second such attack on the oil terminal in five days. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇯🇵 BOJ Likely to Hold Rates Steady Amid Middle East Uncertainty
item.description: The Bank of Japan is likely to hold off raising interest rates next week as fading prospects of a near-term end to the Middle East war keep the country's economic and price outlook highly uncertain. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary Election Winner Peter Magyar Names Finance and Foreign Ministers
item.description: Hungarian election winner Peter Magyar nominated key ministers including Andras Karman as finance minister and Anita Orban as foreign minister for his incoming government. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: 🇰🇷🇺🇸 South Korea-US tensions flare over North Korea intelligence leak
item.description: Seoul's Unification Minister Chung Dong-young rejects accusations he leaked sensitive information about North Korea's nuclear facilities, straining alliance coordination on Pyongyang policy. (Source: SCMP)

item.title: 🇨🇳🇪🇺 China threatens retaliation against EU firms over cybersecurity plans
item.description: Beijing warns of broad retaliation if Huawei, ZTE and other Chinese companies are penalized under proposed EU cybersecurity regulations, escalating tech tensions. (Source: SCMP)

item.title: 🇱🇧💔 UN reports 350,000 displaced and 177 children killed in Lebanon since March
item.description: UN peacekeepers continue observing Israeli artillery fire in southern Lebanon as humanitarian crisis deepens with over 1 million displaced facing worsening conditions. (Source: Anadolu)

item.title: 🇦🇫🇪🇺 EU to host Taliban officials for talks on deporting Afghans
item.description: Taliban officials expected in Brussels for negotiations on returning Afghan migrants to their home country, marking significant diplomatic engagement with the unrecognized government. (Source: France 24)

item.title: 🇷🇴⚠️ Romania plunges into political turmoil as Social Democrats move to topple PM
item.description: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan faces ouster after coalition partners accuse him of destroying their social base with austerity measures, deepening Europe's governance instability. (Source: Politico)

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan issues tsunami warnings after 7.7 magnitude earthquake strikes northern coast
item.description: A powerful 7.7 earthquake hit off northern Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and evacuation orders. Meteorological agency warns of possible stronger aftershocks in the coming week. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canadian PM Carney says economic ties with US are weakness that must be corrected
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Canada must diversify trade relationships and reduce dependence on the US economy, signaling a major shift in Canadian economic policy amid tensions. (Source: AP News)

item.title: 🇳🇿 New Zealand declares state of emergency as severe floods hit Wellington region
item.description: Widespread flooding in Wellington has submerged vehicles, uprooted trees and triggered landslides. State of emergency declared as cleanup begins. (Source: BBC)

item.title: 🚨 Around 250 Rohingya refugees missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
item.description: A vessel carrying Rohingya refugees capsized in the Andaman Sea, with approximately 250 people reported missing in the latest tragedy involving the persecuted minority. (Source: The Guardian)

item.title: 🇮🇳 At least 21 killed as bus crashes into Kashmir gorge
item.description: An overcrowded bus skidded off a mountain road and fell into a gorge in Indian-administered Kashmir, killing at least 21 passengers and injuring dozens more. (Source: Al Jazeera)
